quizzes.label: Question 1
quizzes.question: At the halftime break of Game 7, which team held the lead and by how many points?
options: The Golden State Warriors led by seven points
The Cleveland Cavaliers led by seven points
The game was tied at halftime
The Golden State Warriors led by ten points

quizzes.label: Question 2
quizzes.question: True or False: In the third quarter, Kyrie Irving hit a three-pointer that gave Cleveland their largest lead of the game.
options: True
False

quizzes.label: Question 3
quizzes.question: Who scored the decisive game-winning three-pointer while being defended by Steph Curry in the final seconds?
options: LeBron James
Kyrie Irving
Draymond Green
Tyronn Lue

quizzes.label: Question 4
quizzes.question: The victory in 2016 ended how many years of championship frustration for the city of Cleveland?
options: 19 years
31 years
52 years
73 years

quizzes.label: Question 5
quizzes.question: True or False: LeBron James referred to Coach Tyronn Lue as the final piece of the 16-piece puzzle that completed the team's championship journey.
options: True
False

quizzes.label: Question 1
quizzes.question: Who are AVL trees named after?
options: Adelson-Velsky and Landis
Allen, Victor, and Landis
Adelson, Von-Neumann, and Lewis
Armstrong, Vance, and Lee

quizzes.label: Question 2
quizzes.question: How is the balance factor of a node in an AVL tree calculated based on the video description?
options: Right subtree height minus left subtree height
Total number of nodes divided by height
Left subtree height minus right subtree height
The height of the root minus the height of the leaf

quizzes.label: Question 3
quizzes.question: Which of the following sets of values represent the only valid balance factors for a node in an AVL tree?
options: Any value between -2 and +2
Only 0

quizzes.label: Question 4
quizzes.question: If a node is found to have a balance factor of -2, what does this indicate about the tree?
options: The tree is perfectly balanced
The tree is a Red-Black tree
It is not a valid AVL tree and violates the balance condition
The tree needs to be deleted

quizzes.label: Question 5
quizzes.question: Why are AVL trees considered more efficient than Red-Black trees for lookup-intensive (read-heavy) applications?
options: They have a slower search time complexity
They are more strictly balanced, resulting in a smaller maximum height
They do not require any rebalancing during insertions
They use a linear search instead of a binary search

quizzes.label: Question 6
quizzes.question: In the provided code implementation, what is the initial height assigned to a new node upon creation?

quizzes.label: Balance Factor Definition
quizzes.question: What is the specific constraint for a balance factor to be considered valid in an AVL tree?
options: The value must be exactly 0
The absolute value must not exceed 1
The value must be greater than 1
The value must be an even number

quizzes.label: Deleting Nodes with Two Children
quizzes.question: According to the video, which node is used as a replacement when deleting a node with two children?
options: The in-order successor
The root node
The left-most leaf of the entire tree
The node's parent

quizzes.label: Imbalance Detection
quizzes.question: In the demonstration, what violation occurs after node 120 is replaced by 125?
options: The tree becomes a linked list
Node 125 gains a balance factor of 2
The tree height becomes zero
All nodes lose their balance factors

quizzes.label: Rotation Decision Logic
quizzes.question: What criteria are used to determine whether a single or double rotation is required for rebalancing?
options: The total number of nodes in the tree
The balance factor of the node and its child
The value of the node being deleted
Whether the node is the root

quizzes.label: Applying Rotations
quizzes.question: Which specific rotation is demonstrated to correct the imbalance at node 125?
options: Left rotation
Right rotation
Double left-right rotation
No rotation is needed

quizzes.label: Leaf Node Deletion
quizzes.question: When a leaf node like 10 is removed, what is the immediate next step in the AVL algorithm?
options: Delete the entire tree
Update the heights of ancestor nodes and check for violations
Insert a new node at the root
Change the balance factor of all nodes to 0

quizzes.label: The Deletion Algorithm
quizzes.question: After the target node is removed and replaced, what does the algorithm do while moving up the tree?
options: It updates heights and checks for balance factor violations
It stops immediately at the parent node
It re-inserts the deleted node
It converts the tree into a Binary Search Tree without balance

quizzes.label: Time Complexity
quizzes.question: What is the time complexity of a deletion operation in an AVL tree, and why?
options: O(n) because you may visit every node
O(1) because deletions are instantaneous
O(log n) because the height of the tree is bounded
O(n log n) because of the sorting requirement
